copy.title: End-Clock Corner Backdoor (Disguised)
copy.prompt: Down 2. 6 on the game clock. The corner is denied — no hip tell.
copy.game_context: 4-on-4 half-court. Down 2 with 6.0 on the game clock. Ball at the top of the key; defense is denying the swing.
copy.possession_setup: The ball is at the top of the key. You are in the strong corner. x2 has buried the hip arrow into the lane — the only tell is his hand in the passing lane. x4 is hovering at the nail and has already started half-rotating off help.
copy.decision_moment: x2's hand is in your lane, but the hip arrow is hidden against his body — the read sits on the hand alone. Freeze is 300ms tighter than the base. The game clock is at 6 and the next bounce decides it.
copy.visible_cue: x2's hand is in your lane (no hip tell to back it up). x4 is mid-rotation up from the nail; the rim corridor is open but closing.
copy.best_read: Rip baseline early on the hand cue alone, plant the outside foot, and back-cut hard behind x2 to the front of the rim before x4 finishes the rotation. The lead pass arrives with two seconds to lift — no shot-clock buy-back this time, the game clock is the only clock that matters.
copy.explanation_md: **End-Clock, Disguised.** D4: the denial is read off the hand alone — the hip arrow is hidden, the cognition window is 300ms tighter, and the only clock is the game clock. You don't get a swing-pass reset. The cue you trusted at D3 is still there; you just have less of it and less time. **Read the hand. Rip early. Cut hard before x4 finishes rotating. Commit before the clock crosses 4.**

feedback.correct: Right read at D4. You took the denial off the hand alone, no hip backup, and beat x4 to the rim before the clock decided.
feedback.partial: Relief catch keeps the play alive — but the game clock will decide the next bounce. The disguised denial was the only rim window you get.
feedback.wrong: Read the hand, not the hip. The hip's hidden — the hand is the whole cue.

self_review_checklist: Did I read x2's hand alone (no hip arrow this time)?
Did I rip outside before the freeze compressed?
Did I cut hard enough to beat x4's rotation up from the nail?
Did I commit before the game clock crossed 4?
acceptable_reads: V-cut up to the wing relief — keeps the play alive, but the game clock decides on the next pass and there's no shot-clock buy-back.
bad_reads: Stand in the corner and call for it — x2's hand deflects, ball out of bounds with 5 left.
Front-cut into the paint — x4 was already rotating; the cut dies and the clock runs.

back_cut_baseline_to_rim.label: Rip baseline early and back-cut before x4 finishes rotating.
back_cut_baseline_to_rim.feedback_text: Best read at D4. You took the denial off the hand alone and beat x4 to the rim before the game clock decided.

v_cut_to_relief.label: V-cut up to the wing for a relief catch; live with the clock.
v_cut_to_relief.feedback_text: Okay at D3 pace. At D4 with the game clock, the relief catch hands the next read back to the defense.
v_cut_to_relief.partial_feedback_text: Relief keeps the possession — trades the rim window for one more bounce of clock. The denial was the layup.

stand_in_corner.label: Stand in the corner and call for it.
stand_in_corner.feedback_text: Not yet. With his hand in the lane and 5 left, standing still gives him the deflection and ends the possession.

front_cut_to_paint.label: Front-cut up into the paint.
front_cut_to_paint.feedback_text: Not yet. x4 was already half-rotating; the front cut dies at the rim and the game clock runs out.
